page.title: Marbella Snorkeling Tour by Boat: Best Coves and Gear Tips

data.meta_description: Discover the best marbella snorkeling tour by boat. Explore hidden coves, see marine life & get gear tips. Book your adventure from Puerto Banús today.
data.summary: A guide to the best marbella snorkeling tour by boat, covering top coves like Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés, marine life, gear tips, and our fleet options from Puerto Banús.
key_facts: All charters include licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA.
Top snorkeling coves: Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, and Cabopino – all within 30 minutes of Puerto Banús.
Our fleet ranges from 6.4m speedboats (5 pax) to 29m superyachts (12 pax).
Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 from €749 for 2 hours (skipper + drinks + VAT included).
Mangusta 80 (24m) from €4,719 for 4 hours, includes a free jet ski for snorkeling access.
Best months for snorkeling: June to October (water temps 20–26°C).
Visibility in Marbella coves averages 10–20 metres on calm days.
Gear tips: bring a rash vest (UV protection), reef-safe sunscreen, and a GoPro.

faq.q: What is the best time of year for a marbella snorkeling tour by boat?
faq.a: The best months are June through October, when water temperatures range from 20°C to 26°C. July and August offer the calmest seas and best visibility, often exceeding 15 metres. Spring and autumn can also be good, but expect cooler water and more variable conditions.

faq.q: Which coves are included in a marbella snorkeling tour by boat?
faq.a: Popular coves include Cala del Faro (near Cabopino), Cala Cortés (west of Marbella), and the rocky areas around Punta de la Mona. All are within a 30-minute cruise from Puerto Banús. Our skippers know the calmest spots based on wind and swell.

faq.q: Do you provide snorkeling gear on the boat?
faq.a: Yes, we supply basic snorkeling masks and fins on all charters. For higher-quality gear or prescription masks, we recommend bringing your own. We also have a selection of GoPro mounts available upon request.

faq.q: Can beginners join a marbella snorkeling tour by boat?
faq.a: Absolutely. Our skippers provide a quick safety briefing and choose calm, shallow coves ideal for beginners. Life jackets are available. Many of our guests have never snorkelled before and enjoy spotting fish right from the swim platform.

faq.q: What marine life can I expect to see?
faq.a: Common sightings include damselfish, wrasse, bream, and occasional octopus. In deeper areas near Cabopino, you may see small stingrays or sea turtles. The rocky seabeds around Cala del Faro are home to colourful algae and starfish.

faq.q: How long should I book for a snorkeling-focused charter?
faq.a: We recommend at least 4 hours. This allows time to cruise to two or three coves, spend 30–45 minutes snorkelling at each, and enjoy lunch or drinks onboard. Shorter 2-hour trips work for a single cove if you're short on time.

faq.q: What should I bring for a marbella snorkeling tour by boat?
faq.a: Bring a towel, reef-safe sunscreen (non-spray to avoid damaging marine life), a rash vest or UV shirt, a hat, and a waterproof phone case. A GoPro or underwater camera is great for memories. We provide drinks and snacks.

faq.q: Can I combine snorkeling with other activities on the tour?
faq.a: Yes. Many guests combine snorkeling with paddleboarding (available on request), jet skiing (free on Mangusta 80 charters), or simply sunbathing on deck. Our skippers can tailor the itinerary to your preferences.

data.body_html:
Marbella's coastline hides some of the Costa del Sol's best snorkeling spots, and the best way to reach them is by private boat. A marbella snorkeling tour by boat lets you access secluded coves that are otherwise unreachable by land, with crystal-clear water, rocky reefs, and abundant marine life. Whether you're a first-timer or a seasoned snorkeller, this guide covers the top locations, what gear to bring, and how to book your trip from Puerto Banús.

Why Choose a Boat for Snorkeling in Marbella?

The beaches around Marbella are mostly sandy and shallow, but the best snorkeling happens near rocky headlands and coves. A private boat from Puerto Banús gives you direct access to spots like Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés, where visibility often reaches 15–20 metres on calm days. You avoid crowded shorelines, and your skipper anchors close to the rocks where fish gather. All our charters include a licensed skipper who knows the best spots based on wind and swell conditions.

Best Coves for Snorkeling on a Marbella Boat Tour

Here are the top coves our skippers recommend:

  • Cala del Faro – Near Cabopino, this rocky cove has a small sandy beach and clear water with plenty of fish around the rocks. Depth ranges from 2 to 8 metres.
  • Cala Cortés – West of Marbella, this sheltered cove offers calm water and a mix of sand and rock seabeds. Good for beginners.
  • Punta de la Mona – A rocky headland with deeper water (up to 12 metres) and occasional sightings of stingrays and octopus.
  • Cabopino Reef – Just off the Cabopino marina, this artificial reef attracts bream, wrasse, and small groupers.

All these spots are within 30 minutes of Puerto Banús. Our skippers adjust the itinerary based on sea conditions and your preferences.

Snorkeling Gear: What's Included and What to Bring

We provide basic snorkeling masks and fins on every charter. For a more comfortable experience, we recommend bringing your own gear – especially if you need prescription lenses or prefer a dry snorkel. If you want to capture the underwater life, a GoPro or waterproof phone case is ideal. We also suggest a rash vest or UV shirt to protect against the strong Andalusian sun (SPF 50+ reef-safe sunscreen is essential). Avoid spray sunscreens, as they can harm marine life.

Our Fleet: Which Boat Suits Your Snorkeling Trip?

We have boats for every group size. For a small group of up to 5 guests, the Dubhe (8m) or Mariah SX21 (6.4m) are nimble and easy to anchor in tight coves. For up to 10 guests, the Fairline Targa 12m or Lagoon 380 catamaran offer stable platforms for snorkeling. For larger groups (up to 12), the Mangusta 80 (24m) includes a free jet ski, which is great for exploring shallower areas. All boats depart from Puerto Banús and include a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks.

Pricing for Snorkeling Charters

Our most popular options for snorkeling tours are the Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39, both from €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for 8 hours (includes skipper, drinks, and VAT). The Mangusta 80 (available in grey or white) starts at €4,719 for a minimum 4 hours and includes a free jet ski. For other boats like the Ferretti 94 or Canados 86, pricing is on request via WhatsApp. All prices include 21% Spanish IVA, fuel, insurance, and a full drinks and snacks package.

What to Expect During Your Snorkeling Tour

Your skipper will welcome you at Puerto Banús and brief you on the day's plan. After a short cruise (10–30 minutes) to your chosen cove, the skipper anchors and you can jump in from the swim platform. Snorkeling sessions typically last 30–45 minutes per cove. Between swims, you can relax on deck, enjoy drinks (water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava), and light snacks. Most 4-hour tours visit 2–3 coves. For the best experience, book a full-day (8-hour) charter to explore further afield, including spots near Estepona or Sotogrande.

Best Months for Snorkeling in Marbella

Water temperatures peak from June to October, reaching 24–26°C in August. Visibility is best in July and August, often exceeding 15 metres. Spring (April–May) and autumn (September–October) are also good, with fewer crowds and water temperatures around 18–22°C. Winter months (November–March) are cooler (14–17°C) but still possible with a wetsuit (we can provide one on request).

Book Your Marbella Snorkeling Tour by Boat

Ready to explore the underwater world of the Costa del Sol? Browse our full fleet on the Boat Rental Marbella page, or contact us via WhatsApp for a personalised recommendation. We'll help you choose the perfect boat and cove for your snorkeling adventure.

Frequently asked questions

What is the best time of year for a marbella snorkeling tour by boat?

The best months are June through October, when water temperatures range from 20°C to 26°C. July and August offer the calmest seas and best visibility, often exceeding 15 metres. Spring and autumn can also be good, but expect cooler water and more variable conditions.

Which coves are included in a marbella snorkeling tour by boat?

Popular coves include Cala del Faro (near Cabopino), Cala Cortés (west of Marbella), and the rocky areas around Punta de la Mona. All are within a 30-minute cruise from Puerto Banús. Our skippers know the calmest spots based on wind and swell.

Do you provide snorkeling gear on the boat?

Yes, we supply basic snorkeling masks and fins on all charters. For higher-quality gear or prescription masks, we recommend bringing your own. We also have a selection of GoPro mounts available upon request.

Can beginners join a marbella snorkeling tour by boat?

Absolutely. Our skippers provide a quick safety briefing and choose calm, shallow coves ideal for beginners. Life jackets are available. Many of our guests have never snorkelled before and enjoy spotting fish right from the swim platform.

What marine life can I expect to see?

Common sightings include damselfish, wrasse, bream, and occasional octopus. In deeper areas near Cabopino, you may see small stingrays or sea turtles. The rocky seabeds around Cala del Faro are home to colourful algae and starfish.

How long should I book for a snorkeling-focused charter?

We recommend at least 4 hours. This allows time to cruise to two or three coves, spend 30–45 minutes snorkelling at each, and enjoy lunch or drinks onboard. Shorter 2-hour trips work for a single cove if you're short on time.

What should I bring for a marbella snorkeling tour by boat?

Bring a towel, reef-safe sunscreen (non-spray to avoid damaging marine life), a rash vest or UV shirt, a hat, and a waterproof phone case. A GoPro or underwater camera is great for memories. We provide drinks and snacks.

Can I combine snorkeling with other activities on the tour?

Yes. Many guests combine snorkeling with paddleboarding (available on request), jet skiing (free on Mangusta 80 charters), or simply sunbathing on deck. Our skippers can tailor the itinerary to your preferences.
